4V0H11V21Z\",\"fill\":\"var(--border-faint)\"}]}]]}],[\"$\",\"$L9c\",null,{\"author\":\"$3e:4:props:children:0:props:author\",\"posts\":[{\"title\":\"The Best Open Source Frameworks For Building AI Agents in 2026\",\"description\":\"Discover the top seven open source frameworks for building powerful AI agents with advanced reasoning, multi-agent collaboration, and tool integration capabilities to transform your enterprise workflows.\",\"slug\":\"best-open-source-agent-frameworks\",\"date\":\"April 23, 2025\",\"updated\":\"Feb 24, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/agent-frameworks/agent-frameworks.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"top agent frameworks\",\"best agent frameworks\",\"ai agents\",\"llm agents\",\"agent frameworks\",\"open source ai\",\"autonomous agents\",\"agentic ai\",\"enterprise ai\",\"ai development\",\"langchain\",\"autogen\",\"crewai\",\"multi-agent systems\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What is the best open source framework for building AI agents?\",\"answer\":\"There's no single best framework. LangGraph works well for enterprise apps needing state management and human-in-the-loop workflows. CrewAI is better for quick multi-agent setups with minimal code. Dify suits teams that prefer visual, low-code development. Your choice depends on your use case, team size, and existing tech stack.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use multiple AI agent frameworks together?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Many production systems combine frameworks. You might use LangGraph for orchestration while plugging in Firecrawl's /agent endpoint for web data collection. AutoGen and CrewAI can also coordinate agents built with different tools, and most frameworks support adding custom tools from external libraries.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between an AI agent and a chatbot?\",\"answer\":\"A chatbot responds to messages in a conversation. An AI agent can plan tasks, use tools, make decisions, and take actions across multiple steps without constant human input. Agents built with these frameworks can browse the web, execute code, query databases, and coordinate with other agents to complete complex workflows.\"},{\"question\":\"Which AI agent framework is best for beginners?\",\"answer\":\"Dify is the most beginner-friendly option because of its visual drag-and-drop interface. For developers who want to write code but keep things simple, CrewAI requires the least boilerplate to get a working multi-agent system running. The OpenAI Agents SDK is another good starting point if you're already familiar with OpenAI's API.\"},{\"question\":\"Are open source AI agent frameworks free to use?\",\"answer\":\"All seven frameworks in this list are open source and free to use. The costs come from the LLMs you connect them to. If you use GPT-4 or Claude through their APIs, you pay per token. You can also run local open source models like Llama or Mistral to keep costs at zero, though you'll need the hardware to run them.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I choose between LangGraph and CrewAI?\",\"answer\":\"LangGraph gives you fine-grained control over agent state, memory, and execution flow, which matters for complex enterprise applications. CrewAI is simpler to set up and better for straightforward multi-agent tasks where agents have defined roles. If you need human-in-the-loop approval steps or long-running workflows, go with LangGraph. If you want something running in 20 minutes, try CrewAI.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I add web scraping to my AI agent?\",\"answer\":\"Most agent frameworks support custom tools. Firecrawl provides a Python SDK that you can wrap as a tool for any framework. The /agent endpoint handles dynamic websites with JavaScript rendering, pagination, and multi-step navigation. You can also use Firecrawl's batch scraping and crawl endpoints to collect data at scale before feeding it into your agent's knowledge base.\"},{\"question\":\"Do I need GPT-4 or can I use open source LLMs with AI agent frameworks?\",\"answer\":\"Every framework on this list supports multiple LLMs. The OpenAI Agents SDK works with over 100 models despite its name. LangGraph, AutoGen, and CrewAI all integrate with open source models through providers like Ollama, vLLM, or Hugging Face. Dify supports hundreds of models out of the box. The trade-off is that smaller open source models may struggle with complex reasoning and tool use compared to frontier models.\"}],\"body\":\"$9d\"},{\"title\":\"AI Data Preparation 101: A Complete Guide for AI Practitioners\",\"description\":\"Learn how to prepare data for AI applications with this step-by-step guide. Covers collection, cleaning, transformation, validation, and optimization for RAG systems, fine-tuning, and inference pipelines.\",\"slug\":\"ai-data-preparation\",\"date\":\"Dec 8, 2025\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/ai-data-prep/ai-data-prep.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"ai data preparation\",\"data preparation for ai\",\"llm data preparation\",\"rag data preparation\",\"machine learning data prep\"],\"body\":\"$9e\"},{\"title\":\"Add AI-Powered Data Retrieval to Your Product with Firecrawl\",\"description\":\"Learn how to add web data retrieval to your product using Firecrawl's Python SDK. Covers four patterns with code examples - search, scrape, crawl, and agent-driven gathering.\",\"slug\":\"ai-powered-data-retrieval\",\"date\":\"Mar 03, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/ai-powered-data-retrieval/ai-powered-data-retrieval.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\",\"Web Extraction Mastery\"],\"keywords\":[\"ai search\",\"searching for ai\",\"searching for agents\",\"web search api\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"How is Firecrawl different from a SERP API like SerpAPI?\",\"answer\":\"SERP APIs return search metadata: titles, URLs, snippets. Firecrawl returns the actual page content as markdown or structured JSON. You can also search and scrape in a single call, extract data with schemas, or let the agent endpoint research a topic without specifying URLs.\"},{\"question\":\"Does Firecrawl have a Node SDK?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The package is @mendable/firecrawl-js on npm. It covers the same endpoints as the Python SDK with equivalent methods and response types, so the patterns in this tutorial translate directly to TypeScript/JavaScript.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use Firecrawl for RAG?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. The /search and /crawl endpoints return markdown that's ready to chunk and index. The /scrape endpoint's JSON extraction mode can pull structured metadata alongside the content, which is useful for filtering and retrieval ranking.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I handle pages that require authentication?\",\"answer\":\"The /scrape endpoint accepts custom headers (including cookies and auth tokens) for pages behind login walls. This doesn't apply to /search or /agent, which work through Firecrawl's own crawling infrastructure. For pages that need interactive login (clicking buttons, filling forms), use the browser sandbox instead.\"},{\"question\":\"Does Firecrawl render JavaScript?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. All endpoints render JavaScript by default. Single-page apps, client-side rendered content, and dynamically loaded elements work without extra configuration.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the difference between /agent and /extract?\",\"answer\":\"/extract is in maintenance mode and will be deprecated. /agent is its replacement and does everything /extract did (structured extraction from URLs) plus open-ended research without URLs. For new code, use /agent or /scrape with JSON extraction mode.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I self-host Firecrawl?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Firecrawl is open source and available on GitHub. You can run it locally or deploy it to your own infrastructure. The self-hosted version uses the same API, so your code works with both hosted and self-hosted instances.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I use Firecrawl with MCP?\",\"answer\":\"Run npx firecrawl-mcp with your API key set as an environment variable. This starts a local MCP server that exposes 12 Firecrawl tools (scrape, crawl, search, agent, map, browser, and job management) to any MCP-compatible agent platform. Firecrawl also offers a hosted MCP endpoint if you don't want to run the server locally.\"}],\"body\":\"$9f\"},{\"title\":\"Top 7 AI-Powered Web Scraping Solutions in 2025\",\"description\":\"Discover the most advanced AI web scraping tools that are revolutionizing data extraction with natural language processing and machine learning capabilities.\",\"slug\":\"ai-powered-web-scraping-solutions-2025\",\"date\":\"March 28, 2025\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/automated_data_collection/automated-data-collection.jpg\",\"categories\":[\"Workflows \u0026 Automation\"],\"keywords\":[\"data collection\",\"automation\",\"web scraping\",\"data engineering\",\"ETL\"],\"body\":\"$a3\"},{\"title\":\"Best Hands-On Resources to Learn AI Engineering in 2026\",\"description\":\"Discover 12 hands-on AI resources organized by skill level for building chatbots, RAG systems, and AI agents. Learn AI Engineering with free tutorials from OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, and more, organized from beginner to advanced.\",\"slug\":\"best-ai-resources\",\"date\":\"Nov 21, 2025\",\"updated\":\"Feb 19, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/best-ai-resources/best-ai-resources.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"best AI resources\",\"AI Engineering resources\",\"LLM learning resources\",\"how to learn AI Engineering\",\"RAG development tutorials\",\"AI agent development\",\"learn LLM applications\",\"prompt engineering resources\",\"context engineering\",\"generative AI tutorials\",\"hands-on LLM learning\",\"build with LLMs\",\"AI chatbot development\",\"vector database tutorials\",\"LLM application development\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What's the difference between AI engineering and machine learning?\",\"answer\":\"AI Engineering builds applications using pre-trained LLMs through APIs like OpenAI or Anthropic. Machine learning trains models from scratch with backpropagation and neural network architectures. If you want to ship chatbots and RAG systems, focus on AI Engineering. If you want to research model architectures, focus on ML.\"},{\"question\":\"What's the best resource for complete beginners?\",\"answer\":\"Start with the Prompt Engineering Guide to learn the core skill. Follow with Claude Cookbooks for hands-on API work. Then take DeepLearning.AI short courses for structured learning. All three are free. Work through them sequentially and build a small project with each one before moving forward.\"},{\"question\":\"How long does it take to learn AI engineering?\",\"answer\":\"You can build a basic chatbot in your first week with the beginner resources. Production-ready RAG systems take 2-3 months of focused work. Complex multi-agent systems take 4-6 months. The timeline depends on your programming experience and how much time you spend building actual projects versus just reading documentation.\"},{\"question\":\"Should I learn machine learning before AI engineering?\",\"answer\":\"No. They're different skill sets. ML background helps understand concepts but isn't required to build LLM applications. Start directly with AI Engineering if you want to ship products. You'll learn by calling APIs and building applications, not by studying gradient descent or training neural networks.\"},{\"question\":\"Are these resources free?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. All 12 resources offer free tutorials, documentation, and courses. You won't pay for the learning materials. You will pay for LLM API usage when running your applications. OpenAI and Anthropic charge per API call. Most providers offer free trial credits to get started.\"}],\"body\":\"$a4\"},{\"title\":\"Best Chunking Strategies for RAG (and LLMs) in 2026\",\"description\":\"Compare seven chunking strategies for RAG systems using real benchmark data from NVIDIA and Chroma. Learn when to use recursive splitting, semantic chunking, page-level chunking, late chunking, and LLM-based approaches with practical code examples and honest trade-offs.\",\"slug\":\"best-chunking-strategies-rag\",\"date\":\"Oct 10, 2025\",\"updated\":\"Feb 24, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/best-chunking-strategies-rag-2025/best-chunking-strategies-rag-2025.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"RAG chunking strategies\",\"chunking strategies for LLMs\",\"chunking for RAG\",\"recursive character splitting\",\"semantic chunking\",\"page-level chunking\",\"late chunking\",\"vector database chunking\",\"LangChain text splitters\",\"LlamaIndex chunking\",\"text chunking techniques\",\"document chunking\",\"RAG optimization\",\"retrieval accuracy\",\"embedding chunking\",\"LLM chunking\",\"RAG performance\",\"chunking benchmarks\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"What is the best chunking strategy for RAG?\",\"answer\":\"There's no single best strategy. Recursive character splitting at 400-512 tokens with 10-20% overlap works well for most text content and is the recommended starting point. Page-level chunking performs best for PDFs and paginated documents. Semantic chunking gives higher recall but costs more to run. Your best bet is testing 2-3 strategies on your actual documents and queries.\"},{\"question\":\"What chunk size should I use?\",\"answer\":\"It depends on your query patterns. Factoid queries (names, dates, specific facts) perform best with 256-512 tokens. Analytical queries (explanations, comparisons) need 1024+ tokens for enough context. If you handle a mix of both, start with 400-512 tokens as a balanced middle ground.\"},{\"question\":\"Does chunk overlap matter?\",\"answer\":\"Yes. Overlap (sliding windows) prevents information loss at chunk boundaries. If a sentence gets split across two chunks, overlap ensures both contain the complete thought. Start with 10-20% of your chunk size as overlap. A 500-token chunk should have 50-100 tokens of overlap.\"},{\"question\":\"How is chunking for LLMs different from chunking for search?\",\"answer\":\"Chunking strategies for LLMs prioritize preserving semantic coherence so the model gets enough context to reason about the content. Search-oriented chunking prioritizes precision so each chunk matches a narrow query. In practice, most RAG systems need both — chunks that are focused enough to retrieve accurately but large enough for the LLM to generate a good answer.\"},{\"question\":\"Should I use semantic chunking or recursive splitting?\",\"answer\":\"Start with recursive splitting. Chroma's research showed it delivers 85-90% recall at 400 tokens, while semantic chunking reaches 91-92%. That 2-3% improvement costs embedding every sentence in your documents, which means API calls or local model inference for the chunking step alone. Semantic chunking makes sense when accuracy is your top priority and budget allows for the extra processing.\"},{\"question\":\"How do I chunk code files?\",\"answer\":\"Use recursive character splitting with code-aware separators. Prioritize splitting at class definitions (\\\\n\\\\nclass), function definitions (\\\\n\\\\ndef), then fall back to paragraph and line breaks. This keeps functions and classes intact as single chunks instead of fragmenting them mid-logic.\"},{\"question\":\"What tools do I need to clean web content before chunking?\",\"answer\":\"Raw HTML creates noisy chunks full of navigation elements, scripts, and boilerplate. Firecrawl converts web pages to clean markdown with headers, lists, and tables preserved, giving every chunking strategy better input to work with. The difference matters because clean markdown provides natural split points that structure-aware chunkers can use.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I combine multiple chunking strategies?\",\"answer\":\"Yes, and many production systems do. A common pattern is routing documents by type: PDFs go through page-level chunking, web content gets recursive splitting, and code files use code-aware separators. You can build this routing logic based on file extension, MIME type, or content analysis.\"}],\"body\":\"$a5\"},{\"title\":\"Best LLM Observability Tools in 2026\",\"description\":\"Compare 15 LLM observability tools across four categories. Find the best option for tracing, evaluation, cost tracking, and monitoring your AI applications.\",\"slug\":\"best-llm-observability-tools\",\"date\":\"Dec 2, 2025\",\"updated\":\"Feb 24, 2026\",\"author\":\"bex-tuychiev\",\"image\":\"/images/blog/best-evals-frameworks/best-evals-frameworks.webp\",\"categories\":[\"AI Engineering\"],\"keywords\":[\"LLM observability tools\",\"LLM monitoring\",\"LLM evaluation\",\"LLMOps\",\"AI observability\"],\"faqs\":[{\"question\":\"Which LLM observability tools are actually open source?\",\"answer\":\"Langfuse (MIT license) and Opik (Apache 2.0) are fully open source with no restrictions. Phoenix, TruLens, Evidently, Helicone, Portkey, OpenLLMetry, and Weave have open source versions but may offer additional features in their cloud products. LangSmith, Braintrust, Galileo, Datadog, and New Relic are commercial platforms with free tiers.\"},{\"question\":\"Can I use multiple LLM observability tools together?\",\"answer\":\"Yes, and many teams do. A common setup is a gateway tool (Helicone or Portkey) for cost tracking and routing, paired with an evaluation tool (Phoenix or TruLens) for quality metrics. OpenTelemetry-based tools like Phoenix and OpenLLMetry make this easier since they export traces in a standard format that multiple platforms can consume.\"},{\"question\":\"Should I self-host my LLM observability tool or use a cloud version?\",\"answer\":\"Cloud is faster to start and requires no maintenance. Self-host if you have strict data residency requirements, need to keep prompts and responses on your own infrastructure, or want to avoid per-request pricing at scale. Langfuse, Phoenix, Helicone, and Portkey all have well-documented self-hosting options.\"},{\"question\":\"How much do LLM observability tools cost?\",\"answer\":\"Most tools offer generous free tiers that cover development and early production. Langfuse, Phoenix, and Helicone let you self-host for free. Cloud pricing varies: LangSmith starts at $39/user/month, Datadog charges $8 per 10k requests, and New Relic uses consumption-based pricing tied to data ingestion. Gateway tools like Helicone and Portkey typically charge zero markup on LLM costs.\"},{\"question\":\"Do I need LLM observability if I'm just prototyping?\",\"answer\":\"Not strictly, but adding it early is easier than retrofitting later. A simple gateway like Helicone takes two minutes to set up and gives you cost tracking from day one. When something breaks in production, you'll have the history to debug it.\"},{\"question\":\"Which LLM observability tool works best with my framework?\",\"answer\":\"LangSmith integrates most deeply with LangChain and LangGraph. Phoenix and Langfuse work well with LlamaIndex. For vanilla OpenAI or Anthropic API calls, gateway tools like Helicone and Portkey require just a URL change or header to start logging. If you use multiple frameworks, OpenTelemetry-based tools (Phoenix, TruLens, OpenLLMetry) provide consistent instrumentation across all of them.\"},{\"question\":\"What LLM metrics should I track first?\",\"answer\":\"Start with cost and latency since they're easy to measure and immediately actionable. Add error rates next. Once you have baseline visibility, layer in quality metrics like hallucination detection and relevance scoring. Don't try to track everything at once.\"}],\"body\":\"$a6\"}]}]]}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,":HL[\"/images/blog/resume_parser/demo.png\",\"image\"]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"96:[[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"introduction\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Introduction\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Finding the perfect job can feel like searching for a needle in a haystack. As a developer, you might spend hours scanning through job boards, trying to determine if each position matches your skills and experience. What if we could automate this process using AI?\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"In this tutorial, we'll build a sophisticated job matching system that combines several powerful technologies:\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"children\":\"Firecrawl\"}],\" for intelligent web scraping of job postings and resume parsing\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"children\":\"Claude 3.5 Sonnet\"}],\" for job matching job\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[[\"$\",\"strong\",null,{\"children\":\"Streamlit\"}],\" for a user-friendly web interface\"]}],\"\\n\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Our application will:\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"ol\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Automatically scrape posting against your qualifications\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Send notifications to Discord when strong matches are found\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Provide a web interface for managing job sources and viewing results\"}],\"\\n\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"img\",null,{\"src\":\"/images/blog/resume_parser/demo.png\",\"alt\":\"Screenshot of the AI resume job matching app showing the main interface with job sources sidebar and resume upload section\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"By the end of this tutorial, you'll have a fully automated job search assistant that runs in the cloud and helps you focus on the opportunities that matter most. Whether you're actively job hunting or just keeping an eye on the market, this tool will save you countless hours of manual searching and evaluation.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"If this project sounds interesting, you can start using it straight away by cloning \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://github.com/BexTuychiev/ai-resume-job-matching\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"its GitHub repository\"}],\". The local setup instructions are provided in the README.\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"On the other hand, if you want to understand how the different parts of the project work together, continue reading!\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"overview-of-the-app\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"Overview of the App\"}]]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Before diving into the technical details, let's walk through a typical user journey to understand how the app works.\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The process starts with the user adding web pages with job listings. Here are examples of acceptable pages:\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"https://datacamp.com/jobs\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"https://openai.com/careers/search/\"}]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"https://apply.workable.com/huggingface\"}]}],\"\\n\"]}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"As you can probably tell from the example URLs, the app doesn't work with popular job platforms like Indeed or Glassdoor. This is because these platforms already have sophisticated job matching functionality built into their systems. Instead, this app focuses on company career pages and job boards that don't offer automated matching - places where you'd normally have to manually review each posting. This allows you to apply the same intelligent matching to opportunities that might otherwise slip through the cracks.\"}],\"\\n\",\"$La7\",\"\\n\",\"$La8\",\"\\n\",\"$La9\",\"\\n\",\"$Laa\",\"\\n\",\"$Lab\",\"\\n\",\"$Lac\",\"\\n\",\"$Lad\",\"\\n\",\"$Lae\",\"\\n\",\"$Laf\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lb9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lba\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbe\",\"\\n\",\"$Lbf\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lc9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lca\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lce\",\"\\n\",\"$Lcf\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld0\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld1\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld2\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld3\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld4\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld5\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld6\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld7\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld8\",\"\\n\",\"$Ld9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lda\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldb\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldc\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lde\",\"\\n\",\"$Ldf\",\"\\n\",\"$Le0\",\"\\n\",\"$Le1\",\"\\n\",\"$Le2\",\"\\n\",\"$Le3\",\"\\n\",\"$Le4\",\"\\n\",\"$Le5\",\"\\n\",\"$Le6\",\"\\n\",\"$Le7\",\"\\n\",\"$Le8\",\"\\n\",\"$Le9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lea\",\"\\n\",\"$Leb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lec\",\"\\n\",\"$Led\",\"\\n\",\"$Lee\",\"\\n\",\"$Lef\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf0\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf1\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf2\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf3\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf4\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf5\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf6\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf7\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf8\",\"\\n\",\"$Lf9\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfa\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfb\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfc\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfd\",\"\\n\",\"$Lfe\",\"\\n\",\"$Lff\",\"\\n\",\"$L100\",\"\\n\",\"$L101\",\"\\n\",\"$L102\",\"\\n\",\"$L103\",\"\\n\",\"$L104\",\"\\n\",\"$L105\",\"\\n\",\"$L106\",\"\\n\",\"$L107\",\"\\n\",\"$L108\",\"\\n\",\"$L109\",\"\\n\",\"$L10a\",\"\\n\",\"$L10b\",\"\\n\",\"$L10c\",\"\\n\",\"$L10d\",\"\\n\",\"$L10e\",\"\\n\",\"$L10f\",\"\\n\",\"$L110\",\"\\n\",\"$L111\",\"\\n\",\"$L112\",\"\\n\",\"$L113\",\"\\n\",\"$L114\",\"\\n\",\"$L115\",\"\\n\",\"$L116\",\"\\n\",\"$L117\",\"\\n\",\"$L118\",\"\\n\",\"$L119\",\"\\n\",\"$L11a\",\"\\n\",\"$L11b\",\"\\n\",\"$L11c\",\"\\n\",\"$L11d\",\"\\n\",\"$L11e\",\"\\n\",\"$L11f\",\"\\n\",\"$L120\",\"\\n\",\"$L121\",\"\\n\",\"$L122\",\"\\n\",\"$L123\",\"\\n\",\"$L124\",\"\\n\",\"$L125\",\"\\n\",\"$L126\",\"\\n\",\"$L127\",\"\\n\",\"$L128\",\"\\n\",\"$L129\",\"\\n\",\"$L12a\",\"\\n\",\"$L12b\",\"\\n\",\"$L12c\",\"\\n\",\"$L12d\",\"\\n\",\"$L12e\",\"\\n\",\"$L12f\",\"\\n\",\"$L130\",\"\\n\",\"$L131\",\"\\n\",\"$L132\",\"\\n\",\"$L133\",\"\\n\",\"$L134\",\"\\n\",\"$L135\",\"\\n\",\"$L136\",\"\\n\",\"$L137\",\"\\n\",\"$L138\",\"\\n\",\"$L139\",\"\\n\",\"$L13a\",\"\\n\",\"$L13b\",\"\\n\",\"$L13c\",\"\\n\",\"$L13d\",\"\\n\",\"$L13e\",\"\\n\",\"$L13f\",\"\\n\",\"$L140\",\"\\n\",\"$L141\",\"\\n\",\"$L142\",\"\\n\",\"$L143\",\"\\n\",\"$L144\",\"\\n\",\"$L145\",\"\\n\",\"$L146\",\"\\n\",\"$L147\",\"\\n\",\"$L148\",\"\\n\",\"$L149\",\"\\n\",\"$L14a\",\"\\n\",\"$L14b\",\"\\n\",\"$L14c\",\"\\n\",\"$L14d\",\"\\n\",\"$L14e\",\"\\n\",\"$L14f\",\"\\n\",\"$L150\",\"\\n\",\"$L151\",\"\\n\",\"$L152\",\"\\n\",\"$L153\",\"\\n\",\"$L154\",\"\\n\",\"$L155\",\"\\n\",\"$L156\"]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,":HL[\"/images/blog/resume_parser/discord_alert.png\",\"image\"]\n:HL[\"/images/blog/resume_parser/github_snapshot.png\",\"image\"]\na7:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Each job listings source is added to a Supabase database under the hood for persistence and displayed in the sidebar (you have the option to delete them). After the user inputs all job sources, they can add their PDF link in the main section of the app.\"}]\na8:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"The app uses \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://firecrawl.dev\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Firecrawl\"}],\", an AI-powered scraping engine that extracts structured data from webpages and PDF documents. To parse resumes, Firecrawl requires a direct file link to the PDF.\"]}]\na9:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"After parsing the resume, the app crawls all job sources using Firecrawl to gather job listings. Each listing is then analyzed against the resume by Claude to determine compatibility. The UI clearly shows whether a candidate is qualified for each position, along with Claude's reasoning. For matching jobs, the app automatically sends notifications to the user's Discord account via a webhook.\"}]\naa:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"$\",\"img\",null,{\"src\":\"/images/blog/resume_parser/discord_alert.png\",\"alt\":\"Screenshot of a Discord notification showing a job match alert with details about the position and match analysis\"}]}]\nab:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The app automatically rechecks all job sources weekly to ensure you never miss a great opportunity.\"}]\nac:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"the-tech-stack-used-in-the-app\"}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5\",\"children\":\"The Tech Stack Used in the App\"}]]}]\nad:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Building a reliable job matching system requires careful selection of tools that can handle complex tasks while remaining maintainable and cost-effective. Let's explore the core technologies that power our application and why each was chosen:\"}]\nae:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"1.-,[object-object],-for-ai-powered-web-scraping\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"1. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://firecrawl.ai\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Firecrawl\"}],\" for AI-powered web scraping\"]}]]}]\naf:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"At the heart of our job discovery system is Firecrawl, an AI-powered web scraping engine. Unlike traditional scraping libraries that rely on brittle HTML selectors, Firecrawl uses natural language understanding to identify and extract content. This makes it ideal for our use case because:\"}]\nb0:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"It can handle diverse job board layouts without custom code for each site\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Maintains reliability even when websites update their structure\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Automatically bypasses common anti-bot measures\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Handles JavaScript-rendered content out of the box\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":[\"Provides clean, structured data through \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://pydantic.dev\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Pydantic\"}],\" schemas\"]}],\"\\n\"]}]\nb1:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"2.-,[object-object],-for-job-matching\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"2. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://www.anthropic.com/claude\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Claude 3.5 Sonnet\"}],\" for job matching\"]}]]}]\nb2:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":[\"For the critical task of evaluating job fit, we use Claude 3.5 Sonnet through the \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://www.langchain.com\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"LangChain\"}],\" framework. This AI model excels at understanding both job requirements and candidate qualifications in cont"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"ext. We chose Claude because:\"]}]\nb3:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Handles complex job requirements effectively\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Offers consistent and reliable evaluations\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"More cost-effective than GPT-4 for this use case\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Integrates seamlessly with LangChain for structured outputs\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nb4:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"3.-,[object-object],-for-data-management\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"3. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://supabase.com\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Supabase\"}],\" for data management\"]}]]}]\nb5:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"To manage job sources and tracking, we use Supabase as our database backend. This modern database platform offers:\"}]\nb6:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"PostgreSQL database with a generous free tier\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Real-time capabilities for future features\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Simple REST API for database operations\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Built-in authentication system\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Excellent developer experience with their Python SDK\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nb7:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"4.-,[object-object],-for-notifications\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"4. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://discord.com\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Discord\"}],\" for Notifications\"]}]]}]\nb8:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"When a matching job is found, our system sends notifications through Discord webhooks. This might seem like an unusual choice, but Discord offers several advantages:\"}]\nb9:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Free and widely adopted\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Rich message formatting with embeds\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Simple webhook integration\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Mobile notifications\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Supports dedicated channels for job alerts\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Threading for discussions about specific opportunities\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nba:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"5.-,[object-object],-for-user-interface\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"5. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://streamlit.io\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"Streamlit\"}],\" for user interface\"]}]]}]\nbb:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The web interface is built with Streamlit, a Python framework for data applications. We chose Streamlit because:\"}]\nbc:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"It enables rapid development of data-focused interfaces\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Provides built-in components for common UI patterns\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Handles async operations smoothly\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Offers automatic hot-reloading during development\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Requires no JavaScript knowledge\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Makes deployment straightforward\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nbd:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"6.-,[object-object],-for-automation\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"6. \",[\"$\",\"$L26\",null,{\"href\":\"https://github.com/features/actions\",\"className\":\"!underline\",\"children\":\"GitHub Actions\"}],\" for automation\"]}]]}]\nbe:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"To ensure regular job checking, we use GitHub Actions for scheduling. This service provides:\"}]\nbf:[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Free scheduling for public repositories\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Built-in secret management\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,",{\"children\":\"Reliable cron scheduling\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Easy maintenance and modifications\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Integrated version control\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"Comprehensive logging and monitoring\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nc0:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"This carefully selected stack provides a robust foundation while keeping costs minimal through generous free tiers. The combination of AI-powered tools (Firecrawl and Claude) with modern infrastructure (Supabase, Discord, GitHub Actions) creates a reliable and scalable job matching system that can grow with your needs.\"}]\nc1:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"Most importantly, this stack minimizes maintenance overhead - a crucial factor for any automated system. The AI-powered components adapt to changes automatically, while the infrastructure services are managed by their respective providers, letting you focus on finding your next great opportunity rather than maintaining the system.\"}]\nc2:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"!text-title-h5 [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-72 mb-24 relative\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"JobListings\"}],\" model serves as a container for multiple \",[\"$\",\"code\",null,{\"className\":\"font-mono text-mono-medium\",\"children\":\"Job\"}],\" objects. This model is crucial for the scraper component, as it tells Firecrawl to extract all job postings from a page rather than just the first one it finds.\"]}]\nd4:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"These models form the foundation of our application's data flow:\"}]\nd5:[\"$\",\"ol\",null,{\"children\":[\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"The scraper uses them to extract structured data from web pages\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"The database uses them to store and retrieve job sources\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"The matcher uses them to process job details\"}],\"\\n\",[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"children\":\"The Discord notifier uses them to format notifications\"}],\"\\n\"]}]\nd6:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"By defining these data structures upfront, we ensure consistency throughout the application and make it easier to modify the data model in the future if needed.\"}]\nd7:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"2.-database-operations---,[object-object]\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":[\"2. matching system that combines several modern technologies into a cohesive solution. By integrating Firecrawl for web scraping, Claude AI for intelligent matching, Discord for notifications, GitHub Actions for scheduling, and Supabase for storage, we've created a practical tool that automates the tedious parts of job searching. This allows job seekers to focus their energy on more important tasks like preparing for interviews and improving their skills.\"}]\n155:[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"blog-heading relative [\u0026:not(:first-child)]:mt-48 mb-16\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"absolute -top-[120px]\",\"id\":\"next-steps\"}],[\"$\",\"h3\",null,{\"children\":\"Next Steps\"}]]}]\n156:[\"$\",\"p\",null,{\"children\":\"The modular design of this system opens up many possibilities for future enhancements.
